header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Hybrid or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can enable teams to do their best work—through human-centered, evidence-based approaches.

Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Develop {prog|guides, workflows, and training resources} for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%

Facilitate {prog|training webinars} for 600+ participants, enhancing {prog|program understanding} and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining {prog|program delivery strategy} and operations via {data|feedback analysis}

Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed {prog|learning and development strategies}
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D

Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management

jd.meta: LAIKA Training & Development Analyst — Hillsboro OR

changeLog.note: Tailored for LAIKA Training & Development Analyst. Role leans toward technical-systems training/adoption. ODN: Pair B (focus groups + qualitative analysis) leads — maps to story mapping + gap identification. InGenius: leads with guides/workflow docs (70+ staff, 75% onboarding reduction) + needs assessments (25+ stakeholders) + webinar delivery (600+). NextGen: leads with 2,000+ survey analysis + L&D strategies (swapped from engagement to 'learning and development strategies'). Vestas: leads with onboarding process analysis across 3 locations. Adobe Creative Suite (InDesign) + MS Office + Knowledge Management (Notion, SharePoint) all in canonical skills. Honest gap: no VFX/animation experience, no agile tooling, no pipeline-engineering background — not claimed anywhere. Summary: 'L&D professional' + 'enable teams to do their best work' closing clause.
